purpose: Predicts timing and severity of fiscal deterioration based on old-age dependency ratio threshold crossings at 35% and 45%
components: 35% threshold: triggers 2-4pp structural fiscal deterioration within 15 years (14 episodes, median 3.2pp)
45% threshold: triggers qualitative shift toward monetary-fiscal coordination (Japan, Italy as examples)
Institutional mediators: governance quality, existing fiscal position, pension system design
Effective vs. headline dependency ratio adjustment (4-5pp gap)
Utilization/cost uncertainty dominates demographic uncertainty by 2:1
current_assessment: The 35% threshold framework is well-supported with 14 OECD episodes. The 45% threshold is supported by fewer but more dramatic episodes. US approaching 35% crossing ~2030-2032. The effective dependency ratio gap (4-5pp) identified by challenger_01 is a genuine analytical weakness that may delay crossing timelines.
limitations: Magnitude uncertainty is large (IQR 2.0-5.5pp) even when direction is clear
Effective dependency ratios are not systematically measured across OECD
Healthcare utilization/cost uncertainty dominates demographic uncertainty by 2:1
95% CI on 2050 age-related costs spans 3-8% of GDP
Does not account for AI-driven productivity potential
